pattern: Two running Kadane states, one that has skipped an element and one that has not, track the best subarray with and without a single deletion.
hints: Extend the classic maximum-subarray idea, but carry a second running value for the case where you have already removed one element.

At each index, the best ending here with no deletion follows ordinary Kadane; the best with one deletion is either delete the current element (previous no-deletion sum) or keep it (previous with-deletion sum plus current).
Update both states per element, never letting the subarray go empty, and track the global maximum across both states.

edge_inputs.description: deleting a negative in the middle helps
best is a single positive element, no deletion used
all negative, must keep one element
single element cannot be deleted away
one big negative splitting two positives
delete the only negative to merge runs

stress.description: moderate random array spanning negative and positive values
stress.note: wide range forces the with-deletion branch to matter
